state.name: Connecticut
state.agency_full: Connecticut Department of Energy and Environmental Protection

recent_updates.title: Release-Based Cleanup Regs (RBCRs) — Effective
recent_updates.description: RBCRs took effect March 1, 2026, replacing the CT Property Transfer Act with a release-based reporting and cleanup system.

recent_updates.title: MS4 General Permit — Draft Reissuance
recent_updates.description: CT DEEP issued a Notice of Tentative Determination to reissue the MS4 General Permit; public comment period opened March 25, 2025.

recent_updates.title: REACT Portal Launch
recent_updates.description: CT DEEP's new Release, Environmental Assessment, and Cleanup Tracker (REACT) portal is live for document uploads and tracking as of early 2026.

recent_updates.title: CT Stormwater Quality Manual — Updated
recent_updates.description: Revised manual incorporates updated LID guidance, new water quality storm sizing (1.3"), and updated BMP performance criteria.

documents.title: Remediation Standard Regulations (RSRs)
documents.description: Connecticut’s core soil and groundwater cleanup regulations establishing numeric criteria, exposure assumptions, and compliance pathways for site remediation. The newer RBCRs build on this framework by modernizing risk-based cleanup evaluation and implementation.

documents.title: RSRs — Fact Sheet
documents.description: Plain-language summary of how the RSRs work, when they apply, and the types of criteria (Direct Exposure, Pollutant Mobility, groundwater protection). Good starting point for new users.

documents.title: Release-Based Cleanup Regulations (RBCRs) — Main Page
documents.description: The new CT cleanup regulatory framework effective March 1, 2026. Replaces the Property Transfer Act. Describes requirements to report, characterize, and remediate releases. Incorporates the RSRs.

documents.title: Environmental Cleanup — REACT Portal & Overview
documents.description: CT DEEP's Environmental Cleanup program page, including the new REACT (Release, Environmental Assessment, and Cleanup Tracker) portal for document submission and property tracking.

documents.title: Technical Impracticability (TI) Variance
documents.description: Remedial option under the RSRs when NAPL cannot be contained or removed per RSR requirements and remediation has been conducted to the extent technically practicable.

documents.title: Engineered Controls (ECs) — Overview
documents.description: Permanent physical structures used to isolate pollutants that cannot meet RSR direct cleanup criteria. Application requirements, financial assurance forms, and completion statements available here.

documents.title: Licensed Environmental Professional (LEP) Program
documents.description: Overview of the CT LEP licensure system. LEPs verify site investigations and remediation under the RSRs/RBCRs. Includes program description, LEP roster link, exam applications, and renewal forms.

documents.title: LEP Verifications — Forms & Instructions
documents.description: LEP Verification Forms (Forms III and IV, 133x, Brownfields, RCRA) and instructions. Required for submitting a verification that a site has been remediated per RSRs or RBCRs.

documents.title: Verification Report Guidance Document
documents.description: CT DEEP guidance on expectations for a Verification Report submitted by an LEP. Outlines required content, format, and checklist items consistent with RSR/RBCR requirements.

documents.title: Voluntary Remediation Program (VRP) — CGS 22a-133x Fact Sheet
documents.description: Fact sheet for CT's elective VRP. Any party can file an ECAF and fee with CT DEEP to expedite investigation and cleanup of contaminated property under LEP oversight.

documents.title: Remediation Forms — Central Repository
documents.description: Master forms page for CT DEEP Remediation Division: ECAF, Engineered Control variance forms, LEP verification forms, monitoring status forms, and APS/criteria approval requests.

documents.title: Technical Support Documents — RSR Criteria Derivation
documents.description: Supporting documentation on how CT RSR soil and groundwater criteria were derived, including EPA IRIS toxicity values, exposure assumptions, and criteria for EPH/VPH/APH petroleum methods.

documents.title: MS4 General Permit — Current (Effective Oct 1, 2023)
documents.description: General Permit for Discharge of Stormwater from Small Municipal Separate Storm Sewer Systems (MS4). Requires municipalities, public universities, and state/federal facilities to implement six Minimum Control Measures.

documents.title: MS4 General Permit — 2025 Draft Reissuance
documents.description: Draft MS4 General Permit issued March 2025 for public comment. Notice of Tentative Determination and Fact Sheet available. Permit administratively extended while DEEP reviews comments.

documents.title: Construction Stormwater General Permit (CGP)
documents.description: Required for construction sites disturbing 1+ acres. Requires a Stormwater Pollution Control Plan (SPCP). Locally Approvable projects (1–5 acres) may not need to register with DEEP directly if local approval applies. Effective December 31, 2020.

documents.title: CT Guidelines for Soil Erosion and Sediment Control (2023)
documents.description: Comprehensive guidance for E&S control on construction sites. Referenced in the CGP and required by municipal land-use approvals. Updated in 2023 with new LID integration and construction phasing requirements.

documents.title: Industrial Stormwater General Permit (IGP)
documents.description: NPDES General Permit for Discharge of Stormwater Associated with Industrial Activity. Covers a wide range of industrial sectors; requires Stormwater Pollution Prevention Plans, monitoring, and benchmarks.

documents.title: CT Stormwater Quality Manual (2023 Edition)
documents.description: Primary guidance for post-construction stormwater management in CT. Covers LID site design, BMP selection, water quality volume calculations (updated to 1.3" design storm), and pollutant load reduction targets.

documents.title: CT Stormwater Quality Manual — Online Interactive Version
documents.description: UConn NEMO-hosted interactive version of the CT Stormwater Quality Manual, with searchable chapters, design tools, and workflow checklists for LID and BMP design.

documents.title: Stormwater — CT DEEP Main Page
documents.description: Central landing page for all CT DEEP stormwater programs including MS4, construction, and industrial permits. Access to permit documents, annual report templates, and contact information.

documents.title: Construction General Permit — Proposed Cold Water Habitat Buffer Modification
documents.description: DEEP proposed limited modification to the CGP allowing construction within 100-foot cold water habitat buffers if authorized through a separate state permitting mechanism or DEEP Fisheries coordination.

documents.title: Site Characterization Guidance Document (SCGD)
documents.description: CT DEEP's primary guidance for Phase I, II, and III environmental site assessments. Prescribes a three-phase investigation sequence unique to CT. All CT-based ESAs should follow this document alongside ASTM E1527.

documents.title: Site Characterization Guidance — DEEP Guidance Page
documents.description: CT DEEP landing page for site cleanup and characterization guidance documents, including links to the SCGD, expedited site assessment guidance, and supplemental technical documents.

documents.title: REACT — Release, Environmental Assessment & Cleanup Tracker
documents.description: CT DEEP's new online portal for searching properties with known releases, tracking cleanup progress, and submitting environmental documents. Replaces the legacy Online Document Search Portal for post-February 2026 submissions.

documents.title: Brownfields — PREPARED Workbook: Due Diligence
documents.description: CT DEEP guidance on environmental due diligence for brownfield sites, explaining Phase I, II, and III ESAs in the context of CERCLA AAI (All Appropriate Inquiry) and CT-specific protections.

documents.title: Voluntary Remediation Program — ECAF & Phase II Entry Point
documents.description: The Environmental Condition Assessment Form (ECAF) is the entry document for the Voluntary Remediation Program and often follows Phase I findings. Links VRP enrollment with formal Phase II investigation oversight.

documents.title: LEP Program — Overview for Site Assessment
documents.description: Licensed Environmental Professionals (LEPs) in CT are required to supervise Phase II investigations and verify site conditions under the RSRs and RBCRs. Includes LEP roster and audit program information.

documents.title: ASTM E1527 Standard — All Appropriate Inquiry (AAI)
documents.description: Federal ASTM Phase I standard (E1527) referenced in CT DEEP's SCGD. CT ESAs must comply with both E1527 and the SCGD. ASTM E1903 covers Phase II investigations.

documents.title: CT DEEP Online Document Search Portal (Legacy — Pre-Feb 2026)
documents.description: Legacy search tool for environmental documents submitted to CT DEEP before February 2026. Documents after that date are in the REACT portal. Useful for historical site investigation records.

documents.title: Brownfields Program — Main Page
documents.description: CT DEEP Brownfields Program overview, covering eligibility, available programs (VRP, BRWP, BEP), LEP requirements, and liability protections for prospective purchasers and municipalities.
